summary: Backend-only verification of payslip PDF header fix (tagline visibility). Logged in as HR, resolved msairam963@gmail.com from /api/members/directory, generated a 2026-07 payslip (gross 80000 / net 74880), downloaded the PDF, parsed it with PyMuPDF and rasterised page 1 to sample pixels. All 4 pytest cases passed. Tagline color confirmed rgb(71,85,105) = #475569 at 7.5pt (the fix), text extraction found ENGINEERING STUDIOS + DEDICATED TEAMS + AI WORKFLOWS + PAYSLIP + month + slip_no, logo image embedded on page 1, tagline band has 262 dark pixels (legible). Cleanup deleted the generated slip AND the leftover slip e76332ef-9cc8-4627-b4c6-c183306f8365 from mongo hr_payslips.
critical_code_review_comments: hr_module.py is large (927 lines) — consider extracting the payslip PDF drawing (_draw_payslip_pdf) into hr/payslip_pdf.py for maintainability. Non-blocking.
Tagline fix looks good: bold_font 7.5pt at #475569 gives luminance ~86 (well below the 130 legibility threshold and far from the previous #94A3B8 luminance ~155).
PDF generation is synchronous inside an async endpoint path — for large payloads consider offloading to a threadpool. Non-blocking.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_payslip_pdf_header.py (new pytest file — reusable regression for PDF header + tagline + logo)
seed_data_creation: No seed created. Test payslip(s) auto-cleaned via pymongo delete_many on db.hr_payslips (deleted 2 on first run incl. leftover e76332ef..., 1 on rerun).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable regression pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_payslip_pdf_header.py — generates + downloads + verifies + cleans a payslip for msairam963@gmail.com / 2026-07 in a single run. Uses fitz (PyMuPDF) + PIL for pixel sampling. Extract dict-level color/size from get_text('dict'). Note: tagline is drawn at y=H-30mm; corresponding raster row at 150 dpi is around y_px≈170-210. Cleanup targets both id list AND month+employee sweep.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: tagline drawn at 6.5pt with #94A3B8 fill on a cream (#FFF7ED) header band — contrast ratio too low to be readable. Fix in _draw_payslip_pdf (lines 246-248): bold_font @ 7.5pt with #475569 (dark slate). Verified color, size, embedded logo, and pixel legibility all correct.
